seo.title: Building RAG Pipeline with LangGraph + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build rag pipeline with LangGraph + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building RAG pipelines with LangGraph enables complex, stateful retrieval-augmented generation workflows that combine vector search, context assembly, and LLM synthesis in cyclical graphs. Guardrails are essential due to risks like cyclic execution loops, state corruption from parallel retrieval branches, and hallucinations in source attribution. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure source faithfulness, context relevance, and citation accuracy in production deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: RAG Pipeline Architecture with LangGraph
architectureOverview.description:
A typical LangGraph RAG pipeline models the workflow as a stateful graph with nodes for document retrieval, chunking, vector search, context reranking, and LLM synthesis. The graph maintains a shared state containing retrieved documents, assembled context, and generated responses with citations. Parallel branches handle multi-query retrieval and reranking, merging results before synthesis to enable iterative refinement.

Edges support conditional routing for human-in-the-loop review or fallback retrieval when initial context is insufficient. Checkpoints persist graph state across interruptions, enabling resumability. The `graph.invoke()` method executes the full workflow, but failure modes like infinite cycles or checkpoint deserialization errors can corrupt outputs.

Vex integration wraps critical nodes (retriever, synthesizer) or the entire `invoke()` call to verify source faithfulness and prevent hallucinated citations.

risks.name: Cyclic graph execution causing infinite state transitions
risks.description: Conditional edges for iterative retrieval can create unintended loops when relevance thresholds aren't met, exhausting resources.
risks.mitigation: Add Vex cycle detection to `graph.invoke()` and max_iterations to StateGraph config

risks.name: State corruption when parallel branches merge conflicting data
risks.description: Parallel retrievers returning overlapping documents with different metadata corrupt the merged context state.
risks.mitigation: Verify merged state with Vex before synthesis node using document deduplication checks

risks.name: Hallucination in citation attribution
risks.description: LLM generates plausible but incorrect source references, breaking user trust in RAG outputs.
risks.mitigation: Vex source faithfulness checks on final response with citation trace validation

risks.name: Context relevance drift in iterative refinement
risks.description: Follow-up retrieval branches accumulate irrelevant context, degrading synthesis quality.
risks.mitigation: Per-node Vex verification of context relevance scores before state updates

implementation.title: Basic LangGraph RAG State Schema
implementation.description: Define the shared state schema with documents, context, and citations for graph execution.
implementation.code:
from typing import TypedDict, Annotated, List
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, END
from langgraph.graph.message import add_messages

class RAGState(TypedDict):
    messages: Annotated[list, add_messages]
    query: str
    documents: List[dict]
    context: str
    response: str
    citations: List[str]
    relevance_scores: List[float]

implementation.title: Parallel Retrieval Nodes
implementation.description: Implement vector search and keyword fallback retrievers running in parallel.
implementation.code:
from langgraph.prebuilt import ToolNode
import chromadb

@graph.node
def retrieve_docs(state: RAGState):
    # Vector search
    vector_results = vector_store.similarity_search(state['query'], k=5)
    # Keyword fallback
    keyword_results = keyword_store.search(state['query'], k=3)
    
    docs = vector_results + keyword_results
    return {'documents': docs, 'relevance_scores': [0.9, 0.85, 0.7, 0.6, 0.55]}

retriever_node = ToolNode(retrieve_docs)

implementation.title: Context Assembly and Reranking
implementation.description: Merge parallel results and rerank by relevance before LLM synthesis.

implementation.title: Graph Compilation and Invoke
implementation.description: Wire nodes with conditional edges and compile the executable graph.
implementation.code:
workflow = StateGraph(RAGState)
workflow.add_node('retrieve', retrieve_docs)
workflow.add_node('assemble', assemble_context)
workflow.add_node('synthesize', llm_synthesis_node)

workflow.set_entry_point('retrieve')
workflow.add_edge('retrieve', 'assemble')
workflow.add_edge('assemble', 'synthesize')
workflow.add_edge('synthesize', END)

rag_graph = workflow.compile(checkpointer=memory)

result = rag_graph.invoke({
    'query': 'What is LangGraph?',
    'messages': []
})

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Full Graph Invoke with Vex
vexIntegration.description: Verify the complete RAG output for source faithfulness and citation accuracy.

vexIntegration.title: Vex as Verification Node
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as a graph node after synthesis for inline verification.

vexIntegration.title: Cycle Detection Wrapper
vexIntegration.description: Prevent infinite loops with Vex-monitored graph execution.

cta.heading: Secure Your LangGraph RAG Pipeline
cta.description: Add Vex verification to your production RAG graphs today with one line of code.
